full_text:
- Rajasthan Heatwave: Temp Crosses 46°C | Half State Affected Next 4 Days
Rajasthan scorches with heatwave, nights turn hotter:Sriganganagar remains hottest city, temperature crosses 46°C
- Copy link
People across Rajasthan are struggling with intense heat and heatwave conditions. Most cities witnessed severe heatwave conditions along with scorching sunshine on Wednesday. Temperatures crossed 44 degrees Celsius in Kota, Chittorgarh, Pilani (Jhunjhunu), Alwar and several other cities.
Sri Ganganagar remained the hottest city in the state, where the maximum temperature crossed 46 degrees Celsius. There is little chance of relief from the severe heat and heatwave conditions over the next four to five days. A yellow alert has been issued for heatwave conditions in more than half of the state for the next three to four days.
2 PHOTOS related to Rajasthan's weather
Districts of Rajasthan remained the hottest
According to weather data from the last 24 hours, the highest maximum temperature was recorded in Sri Ganganagar at 46.5 degrees Celsius. Pilani in Jhunjhunu district was the second hottest city, recording 45.3 degrees Celsius.
Temperatures recorded in other cities included 44.8 degrees Celsius in Phalodi and Bikaner, 44.5 degrees Celsius in Churu, 44.2 degrees Celsius in Kota and Alwar, and 44 degrees Celsius in Chittorgarh.
Most cities across the Jaipur, Jodhpur, Bikaner, Bharatpur and Kota divisions remained under the grip of heatwave conditions on Wednesday.
Sirohi, Pratapgarh record temperatures below 40°C
A slight fall of 1 to 2 degrees Celsius was recorded in several cities, including Chittorgarh, Alwar, Tonk, Fatehpur, Karauli and Dausa. In Sirohi and Pratapgarh, temperatures dropped below 40°C on May 20.
The maximum temperature in Sirohi was recorded at 39°C, while Pratapgarh registered 39.6°C.
How will the weather be ahead?
Weather conditions in major cities of Rajasthan
Jaipur: Mercury dips slightly, but heat persists
A marginal drop in temperature was recorded in Jaipur on Wednesday, but residents continued to face oppressive heat throughout the day. The impact of the blazing sun persisted until evening.
According to the Meteorological Department, Jaipur recorded a maximum temperature of 42.5°C and a minimum of 31.1°C during the last 24 hours.
Alwar: Tents pitched on roads to provide shade
Alwar witnessed temperatures close to 45°C for the third consecutive day on Wednesday. In view of the severe heatwave conditions, the Municipal Corporation sprayed water on roads across the city.
Temporary tents were also pitched at various locations to provide shade and relief to passersby.
Udaipur: Roads deserted during afternoon hours
The heat remained intense in Udaipur on Wednesday. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 40.7°C and a minimum of 27.6°C. Although the mercury dipped slightly from Tuesday’s 41.7°C, hot winds continued to trouble residents.
Due to the scorching conditions, main roads and streets in posh colonies remained largely deserted during the afternoon. People stepped out only for essential work.
Ajmer: Intense sunshine and humidity increase discomfort
Ajmer district witnessed sharp sunshine from Wednesday morning onwards. The maximum temperature was recorded at 40.9°C, while the minimum stood at 28.6°C.
The intense heat and rising humidity caused considerable discomfort, resulting in reduced movement on roads during the afternoon. Residents were seen taking precautions to protect themselves from the heat.
Sikar: Heatwave alert for next four days
The Shekhawati region continues to reel under severe heatwave conditions. Sikar district has been experiencing intense heat for the past six days.
On Wednesday, Sikar recorded a maximum temperature of 43°C and a minimum of 24.4°C. Strong sunshine and hot winds since morning made conditions extremely uncomfortable for residents.
Roads remained deserted during the afternoon, with people venturing out only for essential work while covering their faces with gamchas and dupattas. Hot winds have also made nights uncomfortable. According to the Meteorological Centre, weather conditions are expected to remain completely dry over the next four days.
Kota: Temperature crosses 44°C, nights turn warmer
Kota continued to experience severe heat and heatwave conditions on Wednesday. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 44.2°C.
With the minimum temperature remaining around 30°C, residents are now experiencing discomfort even during the night. Due to the blazing sun and intense hot winds throughout the day, roads in the city remained deserted.

title: Sriganganagar hottest at 46.5 degrees, heatwave to intensify in Rajasthan - Punjab Kesari English
full_text:
Jaipur, May 21 (IANS) The Meteorological Department on Thursday warned of severe heatwave conditions in several districts of Rajasthan for the next four days.
Sriganganagar has emerged as the hottest city in the state in the last 24 hours, recording a maximum temperature of 46.5 degrees Celsius. Pilani in Jhunjhunu was the second hottest at 45.3 degrees Celsius.
Phalodi and Bikaner recorded 44.8 degrees Celsius, Churu 44.5 degrees Celsius, Kota and Alwar 44.2 degrees Celsius, and Chittorgarh 44 degrees Celsius.
Most cities in Jaipur, Jodhpur, Bikaner, Bharatpur, and Kota divisions remained in the grip of heatwave conditions.
The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for heatwave conditions in more than half of Rajasthan for the next three to four days, with little relief expected from the scorching temperatures.
Amid the rising heat, preventive measures are being taken in several cities. Water is being continuously sprinkled near Zorawar Singh Gate on Amer Road in Jaipur to reduce surface temperatures.
In Kota, water is being sprayed morning and evening at some cattle shelters to protect animals from the intense heat.
Further, Sirohi recorded a maximum temperature of 39 degrees Celsius, while Pratapgarh recorded 39.6 degrees Celsius, making them among the few districts to remain below the 40-degree mark on Tuesday.
Sirohi, in fact, also recorded a minimum temperature of 21.8 degrees Celsius, making it the lowest temperature in the state in the last 24 hours.
Jaipur recorded a slight fall in temperature on Wednesday, but residents continued to face intense heat throughout the day. The city recorded a maximum temperature of 42.5 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 31.1 degrees Celsius over the past 24 hours.
Alwar continued to reel under temperatures close to 45 degrees Celsius for the third consecutive day. The Municipal Corporation sprinkled water on roads and installed tents at several places to provide shade to pedestrians and commuters.
Ajmer experienced severe heat and humidity from morning. The district recorded a maximum temperature of 40.9 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 28.6 degrees Celsius.
Traffic remained sparse during afternoon hours as residents tried to avoid the scorching conditions.
The Shekhawati region, including Sikar district, has been facing continuous heatwave conditions for the past six days. On Wednesday, Sikar recorded a maximum temperature of 43 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 24.4 degrees Celsius.
Hot winds and rising temperatures have disrupted normal life in most parts of the state, with roads remaining deserted during the peak afternoon hours.
–IANS
